business.headline: Govt eyes stronger forex reserves; FM likely to meet banks soonThe government's move comes at a time when India's foreign exchange reserves have declined sharply. The reserves fell from around $728 billion in February to nearly $682 billion by the end of May.

Home loan on a rented house? Here's what you can still claim under new tax regimeAs taxpayers file their ITRs, understanding home loan tax rules has become more important than ever. In this edition of 'KNOW YOUR TAX', we explain what you can still claim on a rented property under the new tax regime.

TCS, Infosys, HCLTech rise up to 4%: Why are IT stocks rising today?TCS's stronger-than-expected June-quarter results pushed IT stocks higher on Friday. The Nifty IT index climbed 1.82%, while the BSE Focused IT index gained nearly 2%.

Sensex jumps 700 points: Why is the stock market rising today?Around 10 am, the BSE Sensex was trading 800.61 points, or 1.04%, higher at 77,542.43, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 247.80 points, or 1.03%, to 24,210.60.

Sensex, Nifty open in green lifted by IT stocks as TCS earnings cheer marketsThe BSE Sensex opened at 77,395.63 and climbed 676.12 points, or 0.88%, to 77,417.94 in early trade. The NSE Nifty50 rose 196.30 points, or 0.82%, to 24,159.10. The benchmarks had also ended higher in the previous session after recovering from Wednesday's sharp sell-off.

Why TCS may have to rethink its dividend to win the AI raceTCS is winning more AI work, but analysts say scaling it will need heavier investment. That could push the company to rebalance dividends and future growth spending.

EPF Scheme 2026: What it is, who benefits and what it means for youFrom simpler withdrawal rules to new safeguards for retirement savings, the EPF Scheme, 2026 brings several important changes. Here's a quick look at who stands to benefit and what the new rules mean for you.

Tax regime to e-verify: Income Tax Department's 7 golden rules for ITR filingThe Income Tax Department has issued seven golden rules for filing ITRs for AY 2026-27. The checklist urges early preparation, careful verification and timely e-verification to avoid delays and lost benefits.

TCS adds over 9,200 employees in Q1, logs biggest hiring in over a yearAlongside higher hiring, TCS reported a solid financial performance for the June quarter. Consolidated net profit stood at Rs 13,349 crore, while revenue rose 14% year-on-year to Rs 72,275 crore.

TCS Q1 results: Revenue jumps 14%; IT major declares Rs 12 interim dividendThe company posted double-digit growth in both revenue and profit, helped by steady demand from clients, especially in the banking and financial services sector. It also announced an interim dividend of Rs 12 per share for its shareholders.

Sensex, Nifty close higher despite US-Iran tensions; TCS results in focusBenchmark indices closed higher on Thursday as investors returned to beaten-down stocks after Wednesday's sharp sell-off. The rebound was led by midcaps and realty shares, while Middle East tensions, crude prices and earnings remained in focus.

India's insurance industry is seeing a Gen Z takeover, but there's a twistA report says Gen Z advisors are selling more insurance policies than older peers. Experienced advisors still dominate higher-value life cover and long-term protection sales.

Aviva becomes first foreign insurer to fully own India life businessAviva has agreed to buy Dabur Invest Corp's remaining 26% stake in its India life venture. The deal makes it the first foreign insurer to fully own an Indian life business under the new FDI rules.

Old vs new tax regime: Which one will help you save more tax?Before you click 'Submit' on your ITR, make sure you've chosen the right tax regime. A few minutes spent comparing the old and new regimes could translate into substantial tax savings.

TCS shares fall 2% ahead of Q1 results. Can it revive investor confidence?TCS enters the June-quarter earnings season with investors braced for weak spending and softer margins. Its commentary on AI, demand and deal wins could shape sentiment across the IT sector.

Missiles fly, markets rise: Why Sensex is climbing despite US-Iran escalationFresh US strikes on Iran and Tehran's threat have reignited tensions around the Strait of Hormuz, after attacks on commercial shipping. Yet, on Dalal Street, investors are responding with surprising calm.

Sensex jumps 600 points despite US-Iran tensions: Why is market rising today?As of 9:40 am, the BSE Sensex was trading 516.15 points higher, or 0.67%, at 77,019.75, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 139.90 points, or 0.59%, to trade at 24,021.95.

Markets recover despite US-Iran tensions; IT stocks under pressureAt 9:40 am, the BSE Sensex was up 516.15 points, or 0.67%, at 77,019.75, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 139.90 points, or 0.59%, to 24,021.95.

Sensex, Nifty today: Will markets crash again amid rising US-Iran tension?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,995.5 at around 7:50 am, indicating that the NSE Nifty50 could open above Wednesday's closing level of 23,882.05.

US states may sue to block Paramount-Warner Bros merger next week: ReportSeveral US states are preparing to challenge Paramount's proposed acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery in court. The move could delay the deal, deepen financial pressure and intensify antitrust scrutiny.

entertainment.headline: Ikka review: Sunny Deol, Akshaye Khanna courtroom thriller is two decades lateRelease Date:Fri Jul 10, 2026 Ikka movie review: Sunny Deol's Arjun takes up Akshaye Khanna's explosive case after initially refusing it. The courtroom battle ties privilege and justice to a personal family crisis.

You are still my wife: Gaurav Khanna says won't tolerate criticism of Akanksha Actor Gaurav Khanna questioned Akanksha Chamola on Lock Upp over discussing their separation in public. Khanna also said that Chamola is still is his wife and he won't tolerate any criticism against her.

Dhamaal 4 X review: Internet calls it cringe comedy, criticises poor AI visuals Ajay Devgn's Dhamaal 4 has hit theatres, and the first audience reactions on X offer a mixed verdict. While some viewers praised the comedy and Ajay Devgn's performance, others criticised the film's humour and execution.

Korean stars IU and Lee Jong-suk break up after four years of dating: Report IU and Lee Jong-suk have ended their relationship after four years, with both sides confirming the split. They said they would remain on good terms as colleagues while focusing on their careers.

Do Alpha's Sita and Durga belong in the SpyVerse's ultimate boys' club? What happens when Alia Bhatt's Sita and Sharvari's Durga join elite spies Tiger (Salman Khan), Pathaan (Shah Rukh Khan) and Kabir (Hrithik Roshan)? Expect mayhem, high-octane action and a fresh new dynamic in the YRF Spy Universe.

Alpha box office Day 7: Alia Bhatt's film nears Rs 80 crore worldwide in a week Actor Alia Bhatt's Alpha earned Rs 79.21 crore worldwide in its first week, inching closer to the Rs 80 crore milestone. The action thriller collected Rs 2.60 crore nett in India on Day 7, taking its domestic total to Rs 47.55 crore.

Moana review: Dwayne Johnson keeps Disney's live-action remake afloatRelease Date:Fri Jul 10, 2026 Moana 2026 movie review: Disney's live-action revisits the beloved animated adventure with Catherine Laga'aia and Dwayne Johnson leading the voyage. The remake delivers spectacle and sincerity, but its safest choices leave Johnson's Maui doing much of the heavy lifting.

We even thought of separating: Prince-Yuvika break silence on marriage rumours Prince Narula and Yuvika Chaudhary recently spoke about the strain their marriage faced during her pregnancy. They said a misread vlog, public scrutiny and anxiety deepened the pressure before they worked through it.

Aryan Khan attends Raghav Juyal's birthday bash, Shehnaaz joins amid dating buzz Aryan Khan made a rare outing at Raghav Juyal's birthday celebration in Mumbai. Shehnaaz Gill also attended the bash, with their appearance together fuelling ongoing dating buzz after videos from the event surfaced online.

Dada of biopics: Rajkummar Rao is living it up playing the lives of others Rajkummar Rao seems to be emerging as Bollywood's go-to actor for biographical roles. His act as Sourav Ganguly in Dada marks the eighth time he'll play a real-life protagonist.

Mother Promise review: A smart comedy wrapped in absurdity, chaos and madnessRelease Date:Fri Jul 10, 2026 Mother Promise movie review: Dhananjaya's Mother Promise embraces absurdity with confidence, delivering relentless laughs, quirky characters and enough heart to leave audiences walking out of theatres with a smile.

Why Akanksha Chamola is playing the reality TV game better than Gaurav Khanna On Lock Upp, Akanksha Chamola has spoken openly about her separation, her sexuality and her differences with fellow contestants. Her approach stands in contrast to Gaurav Khanna's more guarded run on Bigg Boss 19.

Priyanka Chopra's Heads of State bags Emmy nod for Outstanding Television Movie Actor Priyanka Chopra's Heads of State has been nominated for Outstanding Television Movie at the 78th Primetime Emmy Awards. The film also stars John Cena and Idris Elba in key roles.

Priyanka Chopra gives Nick Jonas a front-row Wimbledon tour on video call Priyanka Chopra attended Wimbledon solo and video-called Nick Jonas from Centre Court. Several pictures and videos of the global icon from the match have emerged online.

Actor Rajesh Sharma's medical statement removed as film body seeks probe The statement shared by actor Sudipa Chatterjee on Rajesh Sharma's medical emergency is no longer available on social media. The development comes as the film workers' body has called for a high-level probe into the circumstances that allegedly led to the actor's hospitalisation.

Jana Nayagan censor cuts explained: TVK reference dropped, cuss words muted The Central Board of Film Certification (CBFC) made several changes before clearing Vijay's Jana Nayagan with an A certificate. Here's a look at every dialogue, visual and reference that was removed, muted or replaced before the film's release.

Actor Sudhanshu Pandey indirectly slams Ssunita Ahuja's conduct on Lock Upp 2 Actor Sudhanshu Pandey questioned the open use of abusive language on reality shows, in remarks seen as an indirect swipe at Ssunita Ahuja on Lock Upp 2. He said normalising insults for entertainment sets a poor example and harms society.

Lock Upp: Gaurav Khanna reacts to Akanksha Chamola's bisexuality revelation On Lock Upp, Farah Khan asked Gaurav Khanna about Akanksha Chamola's bisexuality revelation, and he said he accepted her wholeheartedly. Akanksha said he had been open-minded and understanding.

Evil Dead Burn review: More blood, less scares; mistakes gore for horrorRelease Date:Fri Jul 10, 2026 Evil Dead Burn pushes the franchise's signature gore to new extremes, but is that enough to keep viewers invested? Read our review to find out whether the latest chapter delivers the scares or loses itself in the bloodshed.

general news.headline: I trusted cops, they blamed me for stampede: Vijay in first visit to Karur as CMSpeaking in Karur, Vijay rejected allegations that his party was responsible for last year's deadly stampede, questioning whether the police had adequate crowd control measures in place on the fateful day.

First become an expert mother, then go for IAS: UP Governor's convocation messageAnandiben Patel said women should aspire to become "expert mothers" before pursuing careers as IAS officers or teachers, while urging them to continue education and work after marriage.

Amarnath ice lingam melts just 5 days into the 57-day Yatra. Who's to blame?The Amarnath Yatra, a year after the Pahalgam terror attack, which hit tourism and pilgrimage, has drawn record pilgrims. But the sacred ice lingam in the cave shrine has shrunk dramatically in just five days. Over 50 days of the pilgrimage are still to go. Is climate change alone to blame, or is there more to the story?

When stoppage of Indus waters canal spooked Pakistan; Nehru called act inhumanNearly eight decades before the PM Narendra Modi-led government suspended the 1960s Indus Waters Treaty, the government of East Punjab in 1948 stopped the headworks at the Upper Bari Doab Canal, part of the Indus system. The crisis forced the architect of Pakistan, Muhammad Ali Jinnah, to negotiate with India.

Ikka review: Sunny Deol, Akshaye Khanna courtroom thriller is two decades lateRelease Date:Fri Jul 10, 2026Ikka movie review: Sunny Deol's Arjun takes up Akshaye Khanna's explosive case after initially refusing it. The courtroom battle ties privilege and justice to a personal family crisis.

Rubbish: Gautam Gambhir's oldest insult in England just got a new meaningA year apart, England has once again laid bare Gautam Gambhir's tactical shortcomings. Gambhir is undoubtedly one of the finest T20 coaches around, so why do his teams keep falling apart when the conditions become even slightly challenging?

Dhamaal 4 Movie Release and Review Live: Internet calls it snoozefestDhamaal 4 Movie Review and Release Live Updates: Ajay Devgn's Dhamaal 4 is now playing in theatres. The comedy entertainer, featuring Riteish Deshmukh, Arshad Warsi, Jaaved Jaaferi and an ensemble cast, has finally released, with the first audience reactions and reviews beginning to surface online.

Woman kidnapped during Italy holiday, not once, but twice in a dayAn Australian woman said she escaped two alleged kidnapping attempts by different men in Sicily within hours. The ordeal left her shaken and forced her to put her plans to buy a house in Italy on hold.

Vijay the shock absorber: How TVK chief navigated the Karur nightmareIt's nearly a year since 41 people were killed at Vijay's election rally in Karur. Now the Chief Minister, Vijay is visiting Karur, from where he rushed away after the tragedy. He will give letters of compassionate appointments to family members of 32 victims. Here's how Vijay navigated the Karur tragedy with heart and mind.

Did White House accidentally call JD Vance 'dumb a**' after speech gaffe?A White House post calling a journalist a "dumb a**" sparked online mockery after footage suggested JD Vance's own handling of a poster -- not an altered video -- had obscured the image, leading critics to say the administration had inadvertently insulted the vice president.

Sensex jumps 700 points: Why is the stock market rising today?Around 10 am, the BSE Sensex was trading 800.61 points, or 1.04%, higher at 77,542.43, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 247.80 points, or 1.03%, to 24,210.60.

El Nino's revenge: After 9 days of heavy rain, monsoon to weaken againIndia's monsoon has recovered after nine straight days of above-average rain. Forecasts now point to a fresh lull that could strain newly sown kharif crops.

What made Cardi B obsess over Isha Ambani at fashion show? Hint: Not the BirkinWas Cardi B really obsessing over Isha Ambani's rare Birkin bag at Rahul Mishra's fashion show? Know it from the rapper herself.

Raging monsoon kills over 10, floods cities, cuts off highways; more rain coming Live UpdatesThe fresh bout of rain comes just a day after the southwest monsoon completed its advance across the entire country, signalling that the active monsoon phase is likely to continue over large parts of India in the coming days.

Shreyas Iyer backed amid mounting scrutiny after England T20I series debacleENG vs IND: India assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate has backed Shreyas Iyer despite the team's poor run in T20Is following series defeats to Ireland and England, urging critics to show patience with the new captain as India navigate a difficult phase.

Did Israel's 'kill Trump plot' intel provoke US to launch strikes on Iran?Earlier this week, Israel warned the US of a new Iranian plot to assassinate Donald Trump as Washington launched a barrage of air strikes on Tehran. The timing of the Israeli warning has raised questions if it was the trigger behind the fresh wave of strikes.

Gadkari wanted one ethanol victim, we will present six: Protesters on E20 challengeUnion minister Nitin Gadkari dared critics of the E20 rollout to bring him one person whose vehicle had been damaged due to ethanol-blended petrol. Protesters have accepted his challenge and said they will present the minister with not one, but six such car owners. They have sought an appointment with him.

60 days or leave: H-1B visa employee gets fired weeks after joining GoogleA software engineer on an H-1B visa lost his Google job just weeks after joining, triggering a 60-day deadline to find a new employer or leave the US. His story shows the growing uncertainty many foreign tech workers, especially Indians, are facing amid layoffs.

Mummy, save me: UP teen goes to meet girlfriend. Her family beats him to death19-year-old Shiv Prasad from Kaushambi was allegedly attacked by his girlfriend's family when he went to meet her in Mahua Khada village. Police have registered a case and detained two suspects.

BHU faces Rs 2.65 crore environmental penalty over illegal felling of 33 treesThe National Green Tribunal has extended the deadline for UP Pollution Control Board to recover environmental compensation of over Rs 2.65 crore from Banaras Hindu University for illegal felling of 33 trees.

Pune man gives up window seat to boy, learns heartbreaking reason behind requestA Pune man gave up his window seat to a little boy on a flight. The heartbreaking reason behind the request left him in tears and with a lasting lesson on unseen struggles.

Centre makes high-alcohol medicines prescription onlyThe Centre has amended drug rules to bring high-alcohol medicinal products under prescription-only sale. The change tightens oversight after states flagged their diversion for intoxication.

Congress asks Bhupendra Yadav to take 'moral responsibility' over aides' removalThe Congress stepped up its attack on Environment Minister Bhupendra Yadav, demanding he take "moral responsibility" for the removal of four close aides and invoking Lal Bahadur Shastri's resignation to question accountability in public office.

Jaipur girl jumped to death in school. CCTV shows what happened just beforeNine-year-old Amaira died after she jumped from the fourth floor of her school building on November 1, 2025. The police registered a suicide case with the victim's parents alleging their daughter was bullied at school.

sports.headline: BCCI concerned over India's T20I slump, review planned after England series After back-to-back T20I series defeats against Ireland and England, the BCCI will review India's recent slump, with secretary Devajit Saikia admitting the board is "deeply concerned" by the team's performances.

Harmanpreet Kaur hopes Lord's Test can heal Women's T20 World Cup wounds ENGW vs INDW: Harmanpreet Kaur hopes India's one-off Test against England at Lord's will help the team move on from the disappointment of the Women's T20 World Cup and rediscover its rhythm in the longest format.

Spain vs Belgium quarterfinal: Can Red Devils spring a surprise? Spain and Belgium are one win away from the 2026 FIFA World Cup semi-finals as they meet at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles on Friday. Spain have won all four of their matches without conceding a goal, while Belgium recovered from an uneven group-stage campaign with victories over Senegal and co-hosts United States to reach the last eight.

Shreyas Iyer backed amid mounting scrutiny after England T20I series debacle ENG vs IND: India assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate has backed Shreyas Iyer despite the team's poor run in T20Is following series defeats to Ireland and England, urging critics to show patience with the new captain as India navigate a difficult phase.

England's Quansah served ban, US's Balogun didn't: FIFA decision sparks debate England defender Jarell Quansah will miss the World Cup quarter-final against Norway after FIFA handed him a two-match suspension for his red card against Mexico. Days earlier, United States striker Folarin Balogun escaped an immediate ban after being sent off for a similar challenge against Bosnia.

Thought Roger Federer was alone at Wimbledon? His cheeky reply says otherwise Roger Federer's appearance in Wimbledon's Royal Box during the Championships sparked widespread discussion, with the image of the Swiss legend quickly going viral on social media. Former world No.1 Andy Roddick has now opened up about the moment.

Rubbish: Gautam Gambhir's oldest insult in England just got a new meaning A year apart, England has once again laid bare Gautam Gambhir's tactical shortcomings. Gambhir is undoubtedly one of the finest T20 coaches around, so why do his teams keep falling apart when the conditions become even slightly challenging?

Morocco coach eyes World Cup revenge after second straight defeat to France Morocco coach Mohamed Ouahbi vowed his side would return stronger after their World Cup campaign ended with a 2-0 defeat to France, saying the Atlas Lions would target revenge when the two teams meet again on the biggest stage.

PM Modi, Albanese lay down sports roadmap between India, Australia at iconic MCG Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese on Friday unveiled the India-Australia Sports Collaboration Roadmap at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG), as Australia backed India's bid to host the 2036 Olympic Games.

Big Bash League's opening match to be played in India, announces PM in Australia Australia's premier domestic T20 competition, the Big Bash League (BBL), will make history later this year with its opening match set to be played in India for the first time.

India vs England: All you need to know about historic Lord's Women's Test India and England will make history by playing the first-ever women's Test at Lord's, with Harmanpreet Kaur's side eyeing only their second Test win over England despite the absence of injured opener Pratika Rawal.

Messi's World Cup message takes centre stage at Argentina Independence Day MassFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi's message of unity from Argentina's 2022 World Cup triumph was read aloud during the country's Independence Day Mass as the world champions prepare for another crucial knockout clash.

Haaland's pressure prank? England smile and call it mind gamesFIFA World Cup: Erling Haaland insists England have everything to lose in the World Cup quarter-finals, but the Three Lions believe the Norway striker is simply trying to get inside their heads.

England furious as Quansah red-card ban reignites FIFA consistency rowFIFA World Cup 2026: England have been left frustrated after Jarell Quansah received a two-match World Cup suspension, with former referees questioning why a similar challenge by Folarin Balogun resulted in a far lighter punishment.

Shreyas Iyer makes bizarre excuse after T20I series loss in BristolShreyas Iyer's attempt to explain India's T20I series defeat to England is unlikely to convince many, given how recently this team lifted the T20 World Cup. Speaking after England sealed an unassailable 3-0 lead with a dominant nine-wicket win in the fourth T20I at Bristol, Iyer said India were in a "transition phase" and that mistakes were inevitable.

Djokovic vs Sinner: Can the Serbian recreate Melbourne masterpiece in London?Novak Djokovic and Jannik Sinner renew their rivalry in a blockbuster Wimbledon 2026 semi-final months after the Serbian defeated the World No.1 at the Australian Open.

Kylian Mbappe puts friendship on hold when facing Achraf HakimiFrance vs Morocco, FIFA World Cup 2026 quarter-final: Kylian Mbappe said there were "no feelings" as France knocked out Morocco and close friend Achraf Hakimi to reach the World Cup semifinals. The France captain admitted seeing his PSG teammate after the match would be emotional despite putting friendship aside.

One humiliation too many: Shashi Tharoor blasts India's nightmare run in T20IsENG vs IND 4th T20I: Shashi Tharoor slammed India's poor run in T20Is after England sealed a 3-0 series win with a nine-wicket victory in Bristol. Calling it "one humiliation too many", the Congress MP said he was left "speechless" by the reigning champions' latest collapse.

Too good, too Mbappe: France march into 3rd straight World Cup semi-finalFIFA World Cup 2026: Kylian Mbappe took his time to break Morocco's resistance after a frustrating evening as France eased to a 2-0 win to reach a third successive FIFA World Cup semi-final.

Things fall apart as Shreyas Iyer's India lose second straight T20I seriesEngland sealed the T20I series against India with a commanding nine-wicket win in the 4th T20I, condemning Shreyas Iyer's side to back-to-back series defeats. This is the first time India have lost back to back T20I series since the 2018/19 season.

FRA 2-0 MAR Highlights: Mbappe, Dembele send Les Bleus to the semifinalsFrance (FRA) vs Morocco (MOR) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (FRA 2-0 MAR) France reached the World Cup semifinals with a 2-0 win over Morocco after overcoming first-half frustration. Kylian Mbappe saw a penalty saved by Yassine Bounou, but Les Bleus stayed patient before Mbappe and Dembele showed their quality to secure a deserved victory.

Muchova outlasts Gauff, Noskova hammers Kostyuk to set up all-Czech finalKarolina Muchova saved a match point to beat Coco Gauff in a gripping Wimbledon semi-final and book her place in the title clash. Linda Noskova then defeated Marta Kostyuk in straight sets to set up the first all-Czech women's Grand Slam final.

FIFA referee chief hits out at rigged World Cup claims: Nobody can question integrityFIFA refereeing chief Pierluigi Collina has rejected allegations of bias in Argentina's World Cup win over Egypt, insisting match officials acted independently and defending the VAR decisions that sparked controversy after the last-16 clash.

Quansah handed two-game ban ahead, FIFA ignores Balogun precedentEngland's defensive problems deepened after Jarell Quansah was handed a two-match World Cup ban for his red card against Mexico, ruling him out of the quarter-final against Norway and a potential semi-final despite hopes of an appeal.

Harshit Rana returns from 4-month injury layoff, to be injured againIndia pacer Harshit Rana has been ruled out of the remaining two T20Is against England with a hamstring injury, just weeks after returning from a four-month layoff. Spinner Varun Chakravarthy has also been sidelined with a similar injury.

4th T20I Highlights: Iyer's Men in Blue get walloped thanks to Brook, SaltIndia (IND) vs England (ENG) Highlights 4th T20I: Shreyas Iyer suffered another series defeat as Harry Brook and Phil Salt powered England to a dominant nine-wicket victory in Bristol. The hosts chased down India's target with ease to seal the series, leaving Iyer still searching for his first title as captain.

Wimbledon rigged? Djokovic vs Sinner semi-final timing causes major controversyWimbledon's decision to schedule Novak Djokovic and Jannik Sinner's semi-final as the second match on Centre Court has sparked speculation among fans, with many questioning whether the order of play gives the world No.1 an advantage amid London's soaring temperatures.

Arnav Paparkar's dream run ends after quarterfinal defeat to Jordan LeeArnav Paparkar's memorable Wimbledon campaign came to an end with a 6-2, 7-5 quarterfinal defeat to USA's Jordan Lee. Despite the loss, the Indian youngster announced himself on the big stage with a landmark run to the last eight.

Portugal set to appoint Ronaldo's favourite coach after World Cup debaclePortugal are set to appoint Jorge Jesus as their new head coach following Roberto Martinez's departure after the FIFA World Cup. The move could see Cristiano Ronaldo reunited with his former manager from Al Nassr.

Semifinals Highlights: Linda Noskova sets up final with Karolina MuchovaWimbledon 2026 women's singles semifinals Highlights: Karolina Muchova stuns 7th seed Coco Gauff in a 3-set thriller to reach her maiden Wimbledon final. Muchova outlasts Gauff 12-10 in the final set tiebreak in a game that lasted 2 hours and 35 minutes. Linda Noskova on the other hand, beats Marta Kostyuk in straight sets to reach the final.

France vs Morocco quarterfinal match guide: Can the dark horses stop Mbappe's men?FIFA World Cup 2026: Morocco must find a way to cage in-form Kylian Mbappe if they are to think of beating France in the quarterfinal in Boston. Their task is made doubly difficult by the catastrophic loss of midfield anchor Ismael Saibari to a severe hamstring injury.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
